mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods of stable unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when GDPC1 growth is positive but RSAFS declines, or 60d forward returns are negative in >55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term momentum in equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during monetary tightening periods, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation (Rank IC < 0.01) with INDPRO changes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 310.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures, prompting expectations of tighter monetary policy and reduced real corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in >60% of observations when CPIAUCSL > 310.0, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls reflect a strengthening labor market that supports household income and consumption, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with positive payroll growth, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.3 million units combined with rising retail sales signal synchronized strength in both durable and nondurable demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when HOUST > 1300.0 but RSAFS declines, or 20d forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered co-movement instances.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index below 102.0 coinciding with elevated price levels indicates supply-side bottlenecks, which compresses profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when INDPRO < 102.0, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward tests.
